title: Rigor configuration
description: Configuration schema for `.rigor.yml` and `.rigor.dist.yml`. The committed default lives in `.rigor.dist.yml`; per-developer overrides live in `.rigor.yml` (gitignored). Path-bearing keys (`paths`, `signature_paths`, `plugins_io.allowed_paths`, `includes`) resolve relative to the config file that declares them. The full surface is documented at docs/handbook/01-getting-started.md.

includes.description: PHPStan-style include list. Each entry is a path to another `.rigor.yml`-shaped file whose body is merged under this one (later entries win on conflicts). Paths resolve relative to the file that declares them. Cycles are rejected.

target_ruby.description: Ruby version Rigor parses against (forwarded to `Prism.parse_file(version:)`). Format: `<major>.<minor>` or `<major>.<minor>.<patch>`, or the literal `"latest"`.

paths.description: Files / directories to analyze. Directories are recursed; explicit files bypass the `exclude:` filter.

exclude.description: Glob patterns layered over directory globs. Built-in defaults â€” `**/vendor/bundle/**`, `**/.bundle/**`, `**/node_modules/**` â€” apply on top of user entries and cannot be disabled. Patterns use `File.fnmatch?` semantics (NOT regex).

plugins.description: Plugin gems Rigor loads. Each entry is either a String gem-name shorthand (`"rigor-deprecations"`) or a Hash with `gem:` / `id:` / `config:` keys for a configured plugin.

disable.description: Project-wide rule suppressions. Entries are canonical rule ids (`call.undefined-method`), legacy unprefixed names (`undefined-method`), or family wildcards (`call`, `flow`, `assert`, `dump`, `def`).

libraries.description: Bundled stdlib / stdgem RBS libraries to load (forwarded to `RBS::EnvironmentLoader#add(library:)`). Examples: `csv`, `set`, `pathname`.

signature_paths.description: Project-local RBS sig directories. `nil` (the default) lets `Environment.for_project` auto-detect a `sig/` directory.

fold_platform_specific_paths.description: When `true`, `File` path-manipulation methods fold to platform-aware constants (`File::SEPARATOR` etc.). Default `false` â€” most projects want platform-agnostic results.

cache.description: On-disk cache settings. The cache stores parsed RBS environments, class ancestor tables, and plugin-side artefacts; deleting the directory is always safe (re-populates on next run).

plugins_io.description: Plugin I/O policy. `network: "disabled"` (default) makes `Plugin::IoBoundary#open_url` always raise. `network: "allowlist"` (v0.1.2) consults `allowed_url_hosts:` on every fetch â€” the URL must be HTTPS and the hostname must be on the list. `allowed_paths:` extends the default plugin read root (the project root) with extra absolute or relative paths.

network.description: Plugin network policy. `disabled` denies every URL fetch; `allowlist` permits HTTPS fetches whose hostname is in `allowed_url_hosts`.

allowed_paths.description: Extra read roots a plugin may access via `Plugin::IoBoundary`. Defaults to `[]` (project root only).

allowed_url_hosts.description: Hostnames a plugin may fetch via `Plugin::IoBoundary#open_url` while `network: allowlist`. Exact-match (no wildcards in v0.1.2). Plugins fetching a URL whose hostname is not on the list raise `AccessDeniedError`.

severity_profile.description: Profile that re-stamps each rule's authored severity. `lenient` flags the obvious bugs as errors and downgrades the rest to warnings; `balanced` (the default) follows Rigor's stance; `strict` upgrades every rule to error.

severity_overrides.description: Per-rule severity overrides applied after the profile. Keys are canonical rule ids (`call.undefined-method`) or family wildcards (`call`); values are one of `error` / `warning` / `info` / `off`. `off` drops the diagnostic entirely.

dependencies.description: Opt-in extensions to Rigor's outer inference boundary. Per ADR-10, `source_inference[]` lists gems whose Ruby implementation Rigor MAY walk when a signature contract is missing (or, in `mode: full`, alongside RBS). Inferred returns crossing the gem boundary are wrapped in `Dynamic[T]` with `dynamic.dependency-source.*` provenance â€” never published as ground-truth types and never round-tripped out as authored RBS. Omitted / empty = the existing RBS-or-`Dynamic[top]` boundary.

source_inference.description: Per-gem opt-in entries. Gems not listed keep the unlisted-gem default (no source walk).

budget_per_gem.description: Per-gem catalog cap (method-definition count). When the walker reaches this many catalog entries for a single gem, harvesting stops for that gem and Rigor surfaces a `dynamic.dependency-source.budget-exceeded` warning. Default 5000 (covers Rack, Faraday, Sidekiq); range 1250 â€“ 20000 (0.25Ă— â€“ 4Ă— of the default per ADR-10 Â§ "Budget interaction").

budget_overrun_strategy.description: What the dispatcher should do for receivers in a budget-exceeded gem (ADR-10 5b). `walker_cap` (default, Î±): the walker stops harvesting at the cap; methods past the cap fall through to the engine's user-class fallback path. `dependency_silence` (Î˛): same walker behaviour, but the dispatcher additionally returns `Dynamic[top]` for any call on a class belonging to a budget-exceeded gem (silences `call.undefined-method` for unrecorded methods at the cost of weaker static checking on that gem's surface).

parallel.description: ADR-15 Phase 4c â€” opt-in parallel analysis. When `workers` is greater than zero, `rigor check` dispatches per-file analysis across N Ractor workers. Default `0` keeps the sequential coordinator path. The CLI's `--workers=N` flag and the `RIGOR_RACTOR_WORKERS` env var both override this setting; precedence is CLI > env > config > 0.

workers.description: Number of Ractor workers. `0` (default) runs sequentially; `N > 0` dispatches per-file analysis across N workers. Pool mode is currently bound by file-content triviality (Phase 4b.x worker-side env-build stability is the next sub-phase).

bundler.description: Open item O4 â€” target-project Bundler awareness. When `bundle_path:` is set (or `auto_detect:` succeeds), Rigor walks `<bundle_path>/ruby/*/gems/*/sig/` and adds each gem-shipped sig directory to the loader's `signature_paths`. Conflicts with rigor's bundled stdlib RBS degrade gracefully to `Dynamic[top]` with a one-line warning (per the O7 failure-memo in `RbsLoader#env`).

bundle_path.description: Explicit path to the bundler install root (relative paths resolve from the project root). Typical values: `"vendor/bundle"` for an in-tree install, or an absolute path for a Docker-mounted bundle. When `null` (default) and `auto_detect` is true, Rigor reads `.bundle/config`'s `BUNDLE_PATH:` and falls back to `vendor/bundle/`.

auto_detect.description: When `bundle_path` is unset, search `.bundle/config` and `vendor/bundle/` under the project root. Setting `false` disables bundle sig discovery entirely.

lockfile.description: Explicit path to a `Gemfile.lock` (O4 Layer 3 slice 1). When set (or auto-detected next to the project root via the `auto_detect:` flag), Rigor parses the lockfile and FILTERS the bundle-discovered `sig/` directories so only gems whose `(name, version, platform)` matches a lockfile entry are admitted. Stale gems left in the bundle install tree (out-of-band installs, version drift after `bundle update`) are silently dropped.

gem.description: Gem name (matched against the bundle at walk time).

mode.description: `when_missing` (default): walk gem source ONLY when no signature contract resolves for the call site. `full`: walk even when RBS is present; the analyzer reconciles via the contribution-merging rules in ADR-2 (RBS still wins on conflict). `disabled`: explicitly opt out (matches the unlisted-gem default).

roots.description: Subdirectories within the gem's installation directory to walk. Defaults to `["lib"]`. C extensions and `spec/` / `test/` / `bin/` are hard-excluded by the loader regardless of this setting (per ADR-10).

pluginEntry.description: A single plugin entry â€” either a gem-name shorthand or the full configured form.

oneOf.description: Gem-name shorthand, e.g. `rigor-deprecations`. The id defaults to the gem name without the `rigor-` prefix.

id.description: Plugin id, used as the `source_family` prefix in diagnostics (`plugin.<id>.<rule>`). Defaults to the gem name without the `rigor-` prefix.

config.description: Plugin-specific configuration. Validated by the plugin's `manifest(config_schema:)`. The shape varies per plugin; see each plugin's README for the recognised keys.
